title: rosa maximowicziana var. pilosa
intro: Rosa maximowicziana var. pilosa, also known as the hairy wild rose or hairy fragrant rose, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia, characterized by its fragrant flowers and thorny stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rosa maximowicziana var. pilosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a useful remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to create teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
